query: Forecast FEVER TREE in DVH_EXCL_HD for October 2025

system_A.recommendations:
• Protect distribution weight at minimum 0.42 and target a 5pp increase to 0.47 in DVH_EXCL_HD by October 2025 — weighted_dist is the 3rd-ranked SHAP driver (+0.56) and current penetration of 0.42 leaves meaningful headroom; incremental listings would directly lift the forecast above the 154,373-unit base.

• Investigate and address the volume step-down from recent trend before October 2025 — lag_1 (+2.16, value 184,884) and rolling_mean_4 (+0.67, value 206,904) both signal that recent 4-week and prior-month volumes ran ~25–34% above the October forecast, indicating a projected deceleration; conduct an SKU-level sell-out review by mid-September 2025 to identify whether ranging cuts or retailer de-stocking are driving the gap and intervene with a targeted 10% uplift incentive to key DVH accounts.

• Maintain organic demand support with controlled promotional depth (≤18% promoted volume) for October 2025 — rolling_mean_13 (+0.41, value 205,950) confirms sustained long-run demand strength, meaning baseline velocity is healthy; avoid deep price promotions that would erode margin without incremental volume gain, and instead redirect spend toward in-store visibility (secondary placement, chiller strips) to defend the 154,373-unit forecast floor.

system_B.recommendations:
• Defend weighted distribution in DVH_EXCL_HD by securing at least 2 additional listings in the lowest-penetration stores within the channel before October 2025, targeting a +5pp improvement from the current 0.42 weighted_dist score — distribution is the third-strongest positive SHAP driver (+0.56) and, as DVH_EXCL_HD represents 82% of brand volume, even marginal distribution gains here have outsized impact; with volume forecast to drop ~16% from the lag_1 baseline of 184,884 to 154,373 units, protecting shelf presence is the highest-priority lever to arrest the -7% momentum trend.

• Implement a targeted visibility intervention (secondary placement or chiller-end feature) in the top-20 highest-volume DVH_EXCL_HD stores by mid-September 2025, aiming to recover at least 10,000 units against the October forecast — the rolling_mean_4 and rolling_mean_13 SHAP values (both ~206K) confirm the brand has recently traded significantly above the 154K forecast, indicating a structural volume gap rather than a seasonal norm; as a premium brand at 1.7x category price, visibility investment preserves price integrity better than promotional discounting, which would erode the premium positioning for a mid-tier rank-#12 player.

• Stabilise high-CV volatility (CV=1.24) by negotiating a forward-committed volume agreement with the top 3 DVH_EXCL_HD retail partners covering Q4 2025, locking in a baseline order of ~140,000 units — the month_cos seasonal driver is already applying mild negative pressure (-0.15 SHAP) heading into October, and the brand's high volatility means unmanaged demand swings risk both stockouts and over-supply; a committed baseline reduces forecast error and allows more precise promotional activation without cannibalising the premium price tier.
